author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
text: I'm Gatsby. I thought you ought to know. I'm afraid I'm not a very good host. You see, I usually find myself among strangers because I drift here and there trying to forget the sad thing that happened to me.

author: J.D. Salinger
text: If you really want to hear about it, the first thing you'll probably want to know is where I was born and all that David Copperfield kind of crap, but I don't feel like going into it, if you want to know the truth.

author: Alice Walker
text: Dear God, I am fourteen years old. I have always been a good girl. Maybe you can give me a sign letting me know what is happening to me. I don't know what's going on with my life anymore.

author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
context: Muttering to himself on the street
text: I want to attempt a thing like that and at the same time I am frightened by such trifles. It's all nonsense, all vanity. Am I really capable of that? Is that something serious? No, of course not. It's just a fantasy to amuse myself.

author: Jane Austen
context: Overhearing Mr. Darcy at the ball
text: I could easily forgive his pride if he had not mortified mine. But no matter. He is not the kind of man I would ever consider, and honestly I think the whole evening has been rather tiresome. At least Charlotte was there to laugh with.

author: Albert Camus
text: Mother died today. Or maybe yesterday, I don't know. I got a telegram from the home saying your mother passed away, funeral tomorrow. That doesn't mean anything. Maybe it was yesterday. I feel nothing about it really.

author: Harper Lee
text: My name is Jean Louise Finch but everybody calls me Scout. I can already read and write. My father taught me before I even started school. Miss Caroline doesn't seem to like that very much. I don't understand why knowing things is bad.

author: Vladimir Nabokov
text: You can always count on a murderer for a fancy prose style. I have suffered too much, I think, for any mortal. But the story I have to tell is one that I cannot keep to myself any longer. It consumes me.

author: Margaret Atwood
text: We slept in what had once been the gymnasium. I try to think about the past as little as possible. It doesn't do any good. You can't live in the present when you keep going back to what you lost. But I can't help it.

author: Harper Lee
text: You never really understand a person until you consider things from his point of view. Until you climb into his skin and walk around in it. I know that's hard for you right now, but one day you'll see why it matters.

author: Leo Tolstoy
text: I feel as though something terrible is about to happen, or perhaps it already has and I just haven't noticed. There is something in the way people look at me now. I try to be happy but there's this weight I carry everywhere.

author: George Orwell
text: It was a bright cold day in April and the clocks were striking thirteen. Down with Big Brother. I don't even know why I'm writing this. If they find it, they'll kill me. But I can't not write. Something inside me demands it.

author: Suzanne Collins
text: I volunteer! I volunteer as tribute! Please, not my sister. Take me instead. She's only twelve, she wouldn't survive a day. I'll go. I'm not afraid. Well, that's not true. I'm terrified. But I'll go anyway.

author: William Shakespeare
text: I am too much in the sun, Horatio. My father died not two months ago and already my mother has married his brother. How can she? How can anyone move on that quickly? I feel like the whole world is wrong and I'm the only one who sees it.

author: Charlotte Brontë
text: I am not deceitful. If I were, I should say I loved you. But I declare I do not love you. You think I have no feelings, and that I can do without one bit of kindness, but I cannot live so. Nobody could.

author: Charles Dickens
context: Meeting the convict in the graveyard
text: My father's name was Pirrip, and my Christian name is Philip, but I could never say either properly, so I called myself Pip. Please sir, don't hurt me. I'm just a boy. I have nothing worth taking, I promise.

author: Virginia Woolf
text: What a morning! Fresh as if issued to children on a beach. But something is not right with me. I feel this flutter in my chest, this sense that life is slipping past me while I arrange flowers and plan seating charts. Is this all there is?

author: Richard Wright
context: Waking up in his cramped apartment
text: I can't stand this anymore. Living in this tiny room with my whole family. The rat running across the floor this morning, my mother screaming. I'm suffocating. I need to get out but there's nowhere to go. Every door is closed to me.

novel: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway
context: Talking to Manolin about his fishing streak
text: Eighty-four days now without a fish. But I'm not unlucky, no. I've gone eighty-seven days before without catching one, and then I caught big ones every day for three weeks. Tomorrow I'll go out far. Far out past all the others.

author: Sylvia Plath
text: It was a queer, sultry summer, the summer they electrocuted the Rosenbergs. I'm supposed to be having the time of my life in New York, but I can't get excited about anything. Everything feels flat and meaningless, like I'm watching my own life through a glass wall.

author: Toni Morrison
text: I got a tree on my back. Sweet, that's what it's called. It grows there. They put it there. I used to think I could run from what happened but it follows you. It's in your skin, your bones. Some things you never get free of.

author: Mark Twain
text: You don't know about me without you have read a book by the name of The Adventures of Tom Sawyer, but that ain't no matter. I ain't used to fancy living, and to tell you the truth, I'd rather sleep outside under the stars than in any bed.

novel: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
text: I already know everything about you. I've read your files, your financial records, your emails. I know about your conviction. You're not as clean as people think. Neither am I. But at least I'm honest about what I am.

author: Oscar Wilde
text: I don't want to get old. I know that sounds shallow but it terrifies me. When I look in the mirror now I see someone beautiful, and I know it won't last. Everything beautiful gets destroyed eventually. I wish I could stay like this forever.

author: Henrik Ibsen
text: I have been performing tricks for you, Torvald. I have existed merely to perform tricks for you. You and Papa have committed a great sin against me. It is your fault that I have made nothing of my life. I need to think about who I really am.

author: Khaled Hosseini
text: I became what I am today at the age of twelve, on a frigid overcast day in the winter of 1975. I have been peeking into that deserted alley for the last twenty-six years. I know now that there is a way to be good again. But the guilt never really leaves.

author: Joseph Heller
text: They're trying to kill me. I'm not being paranoid. Every time they send me up there, someone is literally trying to shoot me down. And when I tell people, they say that's war. But it's not just war for me. It's personal. They want me dead specifically.

author: Thomas Hardy
text: Why am I on the wrong side of this great divide? I did nothing to deserve what happened to me. The world treats me as if I'm marked, as though the stain is on my soul and not on theirs. Sometimes I think God has forgotten me entirely.

author: Victor Hugo
text: Nineteen years in prison for stealing a loaf of bread. I am a dangerous man, they say. Every door is shut in my face. I have money now but no one will sell me food or shelter. What kind of freedom is this? I am more trapped than ever.

author: Kate Chopin
text: I would give up the unessential. I would give my money, I would give my life for my children, but I wouldn't give myself. I don't know how to explain it. There's something inside me that belongs only to me and I'm just now discovering it.

author: Anthony Burgess
text: What's it going to be then, eh? There was me and my three droogs, and we sat in the milk bar trying to make up our minds what to do with the evening. I like a good bit of the old ultraviolence. It makes me feel alive, you know what I mean?

author: Amy Tan
text: My mother wanted me to be a prodigy. She had this idea that I could be anything in America. A genius, a concert pianist, a famous actress. When I failed at all of it, I could see the disappointment in her eyes. I was never enough for her dreams.

author: Herman Melville
text: Call me Ishmael. Whenever I find myself growing grim about the mouth, whenever it is a damp drizzly November in my soul, I account it high time to get to sea. It is my substitute for pistol and ball. The sea calls to me when nothing else does.

author: Edith Wharton
text: I've been so busy running from one social obligation to another that I've completely lost track of who I actually am. Everyone sees what they want to see when they look at me. But underneath the dress and the smile, I'm absolutely drowning.

author: Franz Kafka
text: Something has happened to me and I don't understand it. When I woke up this morning, everything was different. I can't go to work like this. My family depends on me. If I can't work, what happens to them? What happens to me? I'm terrified.

author: Toni Morrison
text: I waited a long time to find you. They took me away and I was in the dark for so long. Do you know what it's like to be separated from the one person who is everything to you? I crossed water to get back. I need you to remember me.

author: Toni Morrison
text: I just want to be beautiful. If I had blue eyes, people would look at me differently. They wouldn't walk past me like I'm invisible. Every night I pray for blue eyes, and every morning I check the mirror and they're still brown. Nobody thinks I'm pretty.

author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
text: My dear son, you have no idea how much I worry about you. We haven't heard from you in months. Your sister and I scrape together every kopek we can to send you. Are you eating? Are you well? Please write back. A mother's heart can only bear so much silence.

author: Arthur Miller
text: I'm tired. I'm so tired, Linda. I drove all the way to Portland and back and I couldn't sell a thing. My mind keeps wandering. I almost drove off the road twice today. I just can't seem to keep my focus anymore. Something is wrong with me.

author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
text: I'm paralyzed with happiness! Do you want to know the truth? I've been everywhere and seen everything and done everything. I've had a very bad time, Nick, and I'm pretty cynical about everything. The best thing a girl can be in this world is a beautiful little fool.

author: Emily Brontë
text: I've come back. I know you didn't expect to see me again. Three years I've been away, and every single day I thought about this place and what was taken from me. I'm not the same boy you remember. I've changed. Everything has changed.

author: Zora Neale Hurston
text: I been to the horizon and back and now I can sit here in my skin and be glad. Don't let nobody tell you what your heart is supposed to feel. I spent two whole marriages trying to be what other people wanted. Now I'm just being Janie, and that's enough.

author: Leo Tolstoy
text: My whole life has been wrong. I see it now, lying here. I did everything society told me to do. The right job, the right house, the right manners. And none of it mattered. None of it was real. I'm dying and the thing that terrifies me most is that I never truly lived.

author: Ralph Ellison
text: I am an invisible man. No, I am not a spook. I am a man of substance, of flesh and bone. I am invisible simply because people refuse to see me. It's not my skin that makes me invisible. It's their refusal to look.

author: Betty Smith
text: I want to read every book in this library. I know that sounds impossible but I've already started from the beginning and I'm on the letter B. Reading is the only thing that makes the hunger go away. When I'm in a story, I forget that we're poor.

novel: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
author: Ken Kesey
text: Well, what do we have here? This place looks about as lively as a graveyard. Listen, I'm McMurphy and I'm here to tell you boys that just because they put you in here doesn't mean you have to act like you're dead. Who's up for a card game?

author: Gillian Flynn
text: I'm so much smarter than all of them. They see this perfect wife, this amazing woman, and they have no idea it's all a performance. Nick doesn't appreciate what I've done for him. Nobody does. When I'm gone, they'll finally understand what they lost.

author: John Steinbeck
text: Tell me about the rabbits, George. Tell me about how we're gonna live off the fatta the lan'. I like soft things. I didn't mean to hurt it, honest. It was just so small and soft and I was trying to be gentle but I don't know my own strength.

author: Khaled Hosseini
text: I didn't ask for this life. They gave me away like I was nothing, like a piece of property. My mother was right about one thing at least — the world does not care about girls like me. But somewhere inside I still believe I deserve something more.

author: Fredrik Backman
text: That's a residential parking space! Can't you read? There's a sign right there! What's wrong with people today? Nobody follows the rules anymore. My wife, she would have known what to say. She always knew. I just miss having someone who understood.

author: Markus Zusak
text: My brother died on the train. He was coughing and then he stopped and then he was gone. My mother left me here with strangers. I don't understand why. I don't know these people. I just want to go home but I don't think home exists anymore.

author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
text: When I first came to America, I didn't know I was Black. In Nigeria, I was just Igbo. Here, suddenly I'm a category. People see my skin before they see me. I started a blog about it because I needed somewhere to put all these observations that nobody wants to hear.

author: Yann Martel
text: I have survived 227 days on a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger. I know you don't believe me. Nobody does. But let me tell you, when you're alone on the ocean with nothing but a tiger and your faith, you discover what you're really made of.

author: Ray Bradbury
text: Is it true that long ago firemen put fires out instead of starting them? I never thought about it before. I've been burning books for years and never once wondered what was inside them. Now I've read one and I can't stop. There's a whole world in these pages.

author: Patrick Rothfuss
text: I have stolen princesses back from sleeping barrow kings. I burned down the town of Trebon. I am the one they write songs about. But right now I'm just a man running an inn in the middle of nowhere, and no one knows who I really am.

author: Gail Honeyman
text: I'm completely fine, thank you. My life is perfectly adequate. I go to work, I come home, I eat my meals for one. On Fridays I buy two bottles of vodka. I haven't spoken to anyone outside of work in... I don't know how long actually. But I'm fine. Really.

author: Sapphire
text: I can't read. I'm sixteen and I can't read. They just kept pushing me through school like I was invisible. My mama beats me. My father... I can't talk about my father. But I'm here now and I want to learn. I want to be somebody different than what they made me.

author: Jonathan Safran Foer
text: My dad died on September 11th. I found a key in his closet and I don't know what it opens. I've been visiting every person named Black in New York City trying to find the lock. I know it probably sounds crazy but it's the last thing connecting me to him.

author: Dorothy Allison
text: They stamped illegitimate on my birth certificate. My mama cried when she saw it. She tried to get them to change it, went back to the courthouse over and over. I grew up knowing I was marked from the very beginning, before I even had a chance.

novel: The Perks of Being a Wallflower
author: Stephen Chbosky
text: I am writing to you because she said you listen and understand and didn't try to sleep with that person at that party even though you could have. I need someone to listen. I'm starting high school tomorrow and I'm really afraid of going.

author: Gustave Flaubert
text: Is this really all there is to married life? I read about passion and adventure and grand love affairs, and then I look at my husband eating his soup and I want to scream. There must be something more somewhere. This provincial life is suffocating me slowly.

novel: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
author: Ken Kesey
text: They think I'm deaf and dumb. I let them think that. It's safer that way. You hear a lot more when people think you can't hear. The fog comes and goes and sometimes I'm not sure what's real. But I see everything. I see the machinery behind the walls.

author: Guy de Maupassant
text: I have spent ten years paying off a debt for a necklace that wasn't even real. Ten years of scrubbing floors and washing dishes and growing old before my time. Do you know what that feels like? To sacrifice everything for a mistake you can never undo?

author: Kathryn Stockett
text: I've raised seventeen white children in my life and loved every one of them like my own. But my boy, my Treelore, he's gone now. He was writing a book about what it's like being colored here. Maybe telling our stories is what he would have wanted.

author: Jack Kerouac
text: I first met Dean not long after my wife and I split up. I'd just gotten over a serious illness and everything felt dead. Then Dean came along and suddenly life had this energy, this wild burning need to go, to move, to see everything before it's too late.

author: Margaret Atwood
text: I want to steal something. It's a strange desire for a woman in my position. I want to take something from him, some piece of power he doesn't know he's giving away. Even just a word, an unguarded word. In this world, information is the only currency I have.

author: Kazuo Ishiguro
text: I gave everything to my profession. Thirty-five years of service. I believed that dignity and restraint were the highest virtues. But now, driving through this countryside alone, I wonder if I confused duty with living. I think I may have missed something essential.

author: Jhumpa Lahiri
text: I hate my name. It's not Indian, it's not American, it's just awkward. My parents named me after a Russian author they've never even read properly. I feel like I don't belong anywhere. Too American for my parents' world, too different for everyone else's.

author: George R.R. Martin
text: I used to believe in songs and stories. I thought if you were good and beautiful and kind, good things would happen to you. That's what they teach you. Nobody tells you that the world punishes goodness and rewards cruelty. I've learned that now.

author: Yann Martel
text: I am a Hindu and a Christian and a Muslim. People think you have to choose but I don't see why. Each faith shows me a different face of God. My teachers hate it. My parents are confused. But to me it makes perfect sense. Why limit yourself to one door?

author: Ian McEwan
text: I destroyed two lives with a single accusation. I was thirteen and I thought I understood what I saw but I was wrong. I've spent my entire adult life trying to undo what I did that night. Writing this story is my atonement, but I know it will never be enough.

author: Lois Lowry
text: I can see color now. Nobody else can. They took it away from everyone, along with music and love and pain. The whole community lives in this perfect grey sameness and they think they're happy. But they don't know what happy means because they've never felt its opposite.

author: J.K. Rowling
text: I have spent every day since she died protecting her son. A boy who looks at me with the same contempt his father did. Nobody knows. Nobody will ever know the things I've done or why. I loved her. That's the only truth that matters. Always.

author: Toni Morrison
text: I've been gone ten years and nothing here has changed. They all look at me like I'm the one who left but they're the ones who stayed trapped. I don't apologize for living. I don't apologize for wanting more. If that makes me evil, fine. At least I'm free.

novel: We Have Always Lived in the Castle
author: Shirley Jackson
text: I am Merricat. I am eighteen years old and I live with my sister Constance. Everyone in the village hates us. I like my cat Jonas. I dislike washing myself, dogs, and noise. I have protected us from them for so long, and I will continue to protect us.

author: S.E. Hinton
text: When I stepped out into the bright sunlight from the darkness of the movie house, I had only two things on my mind: Paul Newman and a ride home. I wish I looked like Paul Newman. Things are rough all over, but I keep reading my books and watching sunsets.

author: Virginia Woolf
text: Yes, of course, if it's fine tomorrow. I know how much you want to go to the lighthouse. I understand wanting something so badly that the waiting feels unbearable. That's how life is, darling. Full of waiting and hoping and trying to make beautiful things last.

author: Alice Walker
text: You sure is ugly. That's the first thing I thought when I saw you. But then I looked again and I saw something else. Something underneath. Honey, you don't even know what you got. Nobody ever told you, did they? Well, I'm telling you now.

author: James Dashner
text: I don't remember anything. Not my parents, not my home, not even my last name. I just woke up in this metal box going up and now I'm in a place surrounded by walls that move. Everyone here seems to know more than I do. I just want to know who I am.

author: John Green
text: I'm Hazel. I'm sixteen. Thyroid with mets in my lungs. My mother thinks I'm depressed because I rarely leave the house and I spend most of my time reading and sleeping. But I'm not depressed. I'm dying. There's a difference.

author: Veronica Roth
text: My whole life I tried to be selfless like my parents wanted. But when the knife was in my hand and the bowls were in front of me, my blood fell on the coals, not the stones. I chose bravery over selflessness. My mother's face when I left... I can't stop seeing it.

author: J.D. Salinger
context: Talking to Sybil on the beach
text: Do you know what a bananafish is? They swim into a banana hole and eat so many bananas they can't get back out. They're very ordinary-looking fish when they swim in. But once they get in, they behave like pigs. It's a very tragic thing, really.

author: Emma Donoghue
text: Room is the whole world, that's what I told him. For five years I kept him safe in here, gave him everything I could. But he's growing and he needs the outside. I have to get us out even though I'm terrified of what's out there. I have to be brave for him.

author: Edith Wharton
text: Everything I believed about how life should be lived has been thrown into question by one conversation. She's not like anyone I've ever met. She says what she actually thinks. In our world, that's practically criminal. I'm engaged to May, but my mind keeps returning to her.

author: Miguel de Cervantes
text: I know what the world sees when it looks at me — an old man tilting at windmills. But I see giants. I see injustice that must be fought. Perhaps I am mad, but is it not madder still to see the world's wrongs and do nothing? Sancho, hand me my lance!

author: Kazuo Ishiguro
text: I'm a carer now. I've been doing it for almost twelve years. My donors have always done well. I know what's coming for me eventually, the same thing that came for Tommy and Ruth. But right now I just want to remember. While I still can.

author: Nick Hornby
text: My mum tried to kill herself and I found her. She says she's better now but I can tell she's not. I walk home from school every day terrified of what I'll find. I just want one normal day where I don't have to worry about whether my mum will still be alive when I get home.

author: John Kennedy Toole
text: The modern world is a disaster. These people with their television and their hot dog carts and their complete lack of theology and geometry. My valve is about to close just thinking about it. I'm the only one who sees how far civilization has fallen.

author: Kazuo Ishiguro
text: I wasted so much time being jealous and controlling. I kept Tommy from Kathy because I was afraid of being alone. Now I'm completing and none of that matters. I just wish I'd been kinder. I wish I'd spent less time pretending and more time being honest.

author: John Steinbeck
text: People are so easy to read. Everyone wants something, and once you know what that is, you own them. Love, approval, power — it's all leverage. I learned very early that the world belongs to those who understand weakness. And everyone has one.

author: W. Somerset Maugham
text: I can't just go back to selling bonds and playing golf like the war never happened. I watched my best friend die saving my life. Since then, nothing makes sense. I need to understand why. I need to find something worth believing in, even if it takes years.

author: Esmeralda Santiago
text: In Puerto Rico I knew exactly who I was. Here in New York, I'm nothing. I can't speak the language properly. The kids at school laugh at my accent. My mother says this is opportunity but all I feel is lost. I miss the sound of the coquí at night.

novel: It's Kind of a Funny Story
author: Ned Vizzini
text: I'm fifteen and I checked myself into a psychiatric hospital because I couldn't stop thinking about jumping off the Brooklyn Bridge. Everything in my life looks perfect from the outside — good school, smart kid. But inside, it's like a dark machine that won't stop running.

author: Mitch Albom
text: I'm dying, Mitch. ALS. The doctors say I have maybe a year. But here's what I've learned — everyone knows they're going to die, but nobody believes it. If we did, we would do things differently. So I'm choosing to do things differently while I still can.

author: Susanna Clarke
text: The beauty of the House is immeasurable, its kindness infinite. I have explored hundreds of halls and I know I have barely scratched the surface. Sometimes I think the tides are trying to tell me something. I write everything down so I don't forget. Forgetting frightens me.

author: Daphne du Maurier
text: I came here to forget. That's all. The Mediterranean, the sun, the distance. But memories aren't geographic. They follow you. She follows me everywhere. I thought I could start over but I'm beginning to think some things you never truly escape from.

author: Markus Zusak
text: They're all gone. Mama, Papa, Rudy. Everyone on my street. I was in the basement reading when the bombs came. The words saved me and took everyone else. I don't know how to live in a world where words can save you and destroy everything you love at the same time.

author: Neil Gaiman
text: I'm not scared of you. Well, actually I am, but I'm going to do this anyway. You can't keep the ghost children and you can't keep me. I want to go home. My real home, with my real parents, even if they don't pay enough attention to me. They're mine.

author: Marjane Satrapi
text: They made us wear the veil and I didn't understand why. Yesterday we were free and today we're covered. My mother marches in protests and my uncle is in prison. I'm ten years old and I already know that the world adults built is broken. But maybe we can fix it.

novel: Moonlight (In Moonlight Black Boys Look Blue)
author: Tarell Alvin McCraney
context: As a boy on the beach
text: Why do the other kids chase me? I don't bother anybody. I just want to be left alone. Juan says I can't let people make me feel small, but it's hard when everybody already decided who you are before you even open your mouth. I don't even know who I am yet.
